title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Term Insurance with a Critical Illness Rider
When Mr Anil began a new family of his own, one of the first financial steps he planned, was the purchase of a term insurance plan. He felt relieved that, if an unfortunate event were to occur, his family would be financially guarded if he bought a term plan. However, he understood that a simple term plan may not be enough, when a friend of his, Mr Karthik, was diagnosed with cancer.
Because of his critical illness, Mr Karthik lost his job, and his finances went into disarray. Most of his savings were spent on cancer treatment. This awakened Mr Anil’s eyes to the fact that an unfortunate event may come in different forms. He reached out to his insurance agent and immediately purchased a term insurance plan with a critical illness rider.
Here’s essential information about this rider.
What is a Critical Illness Rider?
A critical illness rider is an optional rider available with your term insurance plan. It may provide a lump-sum pay-out when you are diagnosed with a critical illness. This pay-out may be used by the policyholder to deal with the treatment costs of the illness or any other financial needs cropping up in such a situation. As with other riders, the critical illness rider may be opted for by paying a nominal extra premium.
The critical illness rider may be beneficial to anyone. And if you have a genetic or hereditary inclination towards any critical illnesses, it may be a particularly useful add-on. Along with illnesses, a term plan with the critical illness rider may also provide coverage for major surgeries or treatment for certain medical events.
Usually, critical illnesses, surgeries, and medical events covered under the plan may include cancer (of a specified severity), heart attack (of a specified severity), , kidney failure, major organ transplant, permanent paralysis of limbs, Alzheimer’s disease, and so on. The coverage of each of these may be subject to the terms and conditions mentioned in the policy. [1]
How Does the Critical Illness Rider Work? [1]
A critical illness rider may be available as a benefit-based policy with your term plan. What this may mean is that the rider is supplemental to your term insurance coverage. The premium for the critical illness rider may be calculated differently from that of your term plan.
If the person insured under the term plan with the critical illness rider is diagnosed with a covered illness during the policy term, he/she can raise a claim. The claim may only be processed once the survival period may have passed. The survival period may be referred to as a period after the insured has been diagnosed with the illness. The insured may have to survive this period to be able to receive the benefits of the critical illness rider.
When the survival period has passed, the claim may be processed, and if approved, the pay-out may be made to the policyholder. One may use this pay-out as they see fit. In most cases, the critical illness rider may cease to be valid once a claim has been raised and the benefit paid out.
Why Do You Need Term Insurance with a Critical Illness Rider Benefit?
If you take the critical illness rider with your term insurance plan, it may benefit you in many ways, such as:
Providing support during tough times
Being diagnosed with a critical illness may be a difficult situation to go through. It may not be just the diagnosed person that suffers, but also their loved ones undergoing emotional and financial strain. If it is an earning member struck with an illness, the situation may be even more challenging.
The financial support of a critical illness rider may bring a great level of relief. It may act as a pillar on which the family may depend for financial help. Amongst other things, it may help the family cover daily costs and act as an income replacement.
Allowing for better quality treatment
A term life insurance plan with a critical illness rider may help you get good-quality treatment for the diagnosed illness. As you may know, the treatment costs for serious illnesses can be quite high. And since such illnesses may often take a long time to treat, the bills may continue piling up. Even though you may have some savings or an emergency fund for such situations, it may prove to be inadequate. Furthermore, you may end up exhausting those funds, which may not be helpful in the long run.
In such situations, the critical illness insurance rider may prove to be of immense help. The considerable pay-out from the rider may allow you to access the treatment you want, without worrying about the lack of funds. With the right treatment, the chances of your illness being cured may increase. Your loved ones may also be relieved of financial woes and may be able to focus on your rest and recovery.
Providing tax benefits [2]
According to Section 80D of the Income Tax Act of 1961, the premium of a health-related insurance policy may be used to claim tax deductions. This also applies to the critical illness rider purchased with term insurance plans. If the person insured under the rider is under 60 years of age, the maximum deduction one may claim is Rs 25,000. For those over the age of 60 years, the deduction limit may increase to Rs 50,000. This tax benefit may only be applicable to taxpayers under the old tax regime.
Thus, opting for the critical illness rider may help you save taxes along with letting you enjoy other benefits.
Details to Remember When You Opt for a Critical Illness Rider with a Term Plan [1]
If you are planning to opt for the critical illness rider with term insurance, do keep the following points in mind:
The minimum entry age may usually be 18 years and the maximum entry age may be 60 years for most insurers. However, some insurers may allow the maximum age at entry to be 65 years
The minimum tenure for the rider may be 5 years and the maximum may be 30 years, depending on the base policy as well.
The base plan may also determine the premium payment term and the minimum sum assured you may opt for with the rider
The critical illness rider will cease to provide coverage if the policyholder terminates the base plan.
Critical Illness Rider Exclusions [1]
While critical illness riders  aim to provide you with comprehensive coverage, they may have their set of exclusions as well. Let’s look at some common exclusions you may come across in a critical illness rider.
Passing away during the survival period - If the insured passes away within the survival period after being diagnosed with a critical illness, the claim amount may not be paid out
Diagnosis during the waiting period - If the diagnosis occurs within the waiting period of the rider, the term insurance plan with the critical illness benefit may not be able to provide coverage
Being under the influence of alcohol or drugs - If the insured person is found to be under the influence of alcohol, drugs, narcotics, or any psychotropic substance, without a doctor’s prescription for the same, their claim may not be considered valid, and the coverage may be excluded
Pre-existing conditions - If the critical illness may be a pre-existing condition, as described by the policy wordings, the diagnosis of the illness may not be considered to be within the scope of the rider
Self-inflicted injuries or suicide - The critical illness rider may not cover any medical issues arising from self-inflicted injuries, suicide, or attempted suicide
War and warlike activities - Any medical issues arising with the insured person due to war, civil commotions, strikes, terrorist activities, riots, and so on, may not be covered under the critical illness rider
Do note that this may not be an exhaustive list of exclusions. For a detailed list, you may want to connect with your insurer or refer the policy document.
Things to Consider while Buying a Term Plan with a Critical Illness Rider
To make the most of your term insurance plan with a critical illness rider, it may be vital to select and curate the right plan. To help you in the process, here are some things to consider while opting for this rider:
The number of illnesses covered
As with any other type of insurance plan, the coverage of the critical illness may be considered when reviewing it. A good critical illness rider may cover a wide list of critical illnesses, major surgeries, and medical events. Also, note the conditions under which the coverage may become valid. The more comprehensive the coverage, the better the plan may be.
Premiums
Since you may already be paying the term insurance premium, you may not want the premium of a critical illness rider to be too high. Otherwise, it may burden you in the long run. Hence, do select a plan offering you an affordable premium while providing good coverage.
Waiting period and survival period
These two tenures may play a big role in whether you receive the benefits of the critical illness rider. Therefore, you may want to make sure that the waiting period and the survival period are reasonable and allow you to claim rider benefits while following policy terms and conditions.
Conclusion
A term insurance plan with a critical illness rider may be a great way to safeguard yourself and your family against the diagnosis of a critical illness or medical event. While you may not be able to predict if and when you may be diagnosed with an illness or require major surgery, the least you may be able to do is protect your finances against such an event. You may consult with a financial advisor so that you may take an ideal decision for yourself and your loved ones.
Sources:
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/content/dam/balic/pdf/riders/trad-critical-illness-benefit-rider-brochure.pdf
https://cleartax.in/s/medical-insurance#:~:text=This%20deduction%20is%20also%20available%20for%20top%2Dup%20health%20plans%20and%20critical%20illness%20plans.
Disclaimers:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Term Insurance with a Critical Illness Rider When Mr Anil began a new family of his own, one of the first financial steps he planned, was the purchase of a term insurance plan. He felt relieved that, if an unfortunate event were to occur, his family would be financially guarded if he bought a term plan. However, he understood that a simple term plan may not be enough, when a friend of his, Mr Karthik, was diagnosed with cancer. Because of his critical illness, Mr Karthik lost his job, and his finances went into disarray. Most of his savings were spent on cancer treatment. This awakened Mr Anil’s eyes to the fact that an unfortunate event may come in different forms. He reached out to his insurance agent and immediately purchased a term insurance plan with a critical illness rider. Here’s essential information about this rider. What is a Critical Illness Rider? A critical illness rider is an optional rider available with your term insurance plan. It may provide a lump-sum pay-out when you are diagnosed with a critical illness. This pay-out may be used by the policyholder to deal with the treatment costs of the illness or any other financial needs cropping up in such a situation. As with other riders, the critical illness rider may be opted for by paying a nominal extra premium. The critical illness rider may be beneficial to anyone. And if you have a genetic or hereditary inclination towards any critical illnesses, it may be a particularly useful add-on. Along with illnesses, a term plan with the critical illness rider may also provide coverage for major surgeries or treatment for certain medical events. Some of the common illnesses covered by Critical Illness riders are Cancer of Specified Severity, First Heart Attack of Specified Severity, Kidney Failure requiring regular dialysis, Major Organ/ Bone Marrow Transplant and Stroke resulting in Permanent symptoms. However, the actual CIs covered by riders will depend on rider terms and conditions. How Does the Critical Illness Rider Work? [1] A critical illness rider may be available as a benefit-based policy with your term plan. What this may mean is that the rider is supplemental to your term insurance coverage. The premium for the critical illness rider may be calculated differently from that of your term plan. If the person insured under the term plan with the critical illness rider is diagnosed with a covered illness during the policy term, he/she can raise a claim. The claim may only be processed once the survival period may have passed. The survival period may be referred to as a period after the insured has been diagnosed with the illness. The insured may have to survive this period to be able to receive the benefits of the critical illness rider. When the survival period has passed, the claim may be processed, and if approved, the pay-out may be made to the policyholder. One may use this pay-out as they see fit. In most cases, the critical illness rider may cease to be valid once a claim has been raised and the benefit paid out. Why Do You Need a Critical Illness Rider with A Term Insurance Benefit?
If you take the critical illness rider with your term insurance plan, it may benefit you in many ways, such as:  Providing support during tough times Being diagnosed with a critical illness may be a difficult situation to go through. It may not be just the diagnosed person that suffers, but also their loved ones undergoing emotional and financial strain. If it is an earning member struck with an illness, the situation may be even more challenging. The financial support of a critical illness rider may bring a great level of relief. It may act as a pillar on which the family may depend for financial help. Amongst other things, it may help the family cover daily costs and act as an income replacement.  Allowing for better quality treatment A term life insurance plan with a critical illness rider may help you get good-quality treatment for the diagnosed illness. As you may know, the treatment costs for serious illnesses can be quite high. And since such illnesses may often take a long time to treat, the bills may continue piling up. Even though you may have some savings or an emergency fund for such situations, it may prove to be inadequate. Furthermore, you may end up exhausting those funds, which may not be helpful in the long run. In such situations, the critical illness insurance rider may prove to be of immense help. The considerable pay-out from the rider may allow you to access the treatment you want, without worrying about the lack of funds. With the right treatment, the chances of your illness being cured may increase. Your loved ones may also be relieved of financial woes and may be able to focus on your rest and recovery.  Providing tax benefits [2] According to Section 80D of the Income Tax Act of 1961, the premium of a health-related insurance policy may be used to claim tax deductions. This also applies to the critical illness rider purchased with term insurance plans. If the person insured under the rider is under 60 years of age, the maximum deduction one may claim is Rs 25,000. For those over the age of 60 years, the deduction limit may increase to Rs 50,000. This tax benefit may only be applicable to taxpayers under the old tax regime. Thus, opting for the critical illness rider may help you save taxes along with letting you enjoy other benefits. Details to Remember When You Opt for a Critical Illness Rider with a Term Plan [1] If you are planning to opt for the critical illness rider with term insurance, do keep the following points in mind:  The minimum entry age may usually be 18 years and the maximum entry age may be 60 years for most insurers. However, some insurers may allow the maximum age at entry to be 65 years  The minimum tenure for the rider may be 5 years and the maximum may be 30 years, depending on the base policy as well.  The base plan may also determine the premium payment term and the minimum sum assured you may opt for with the rider  The critical illness rider will cease to provide coverage if the policyholder terminates the base plan.
Things to Consider while Buying a Term Plan with a Critical Illness Rider To make the most of your term insurance plan with a critical illness rider, it may be vital to select and curate the suitable plan. To help you in the process, here are some things to consider while opting for this rider:  The number of illnesses covered As with any other type of insurance plan, the coverage of the critical illness may be considered when reviewing it. A preferable critical illness rider may cover a wide list of critical illnesses, major surgeries, and medical events. Also, note the conditions under which the coverage may become valid. The more comprehensive the coverage, the better the plan may be.  Premiums Since you may already be paying the term insurance premium, you may not want the premium of a critical illness rider to be too high. Otherwise, it may burden you in the long run. Hence, do select a plan offering you an affordable premium while providing good coverage.  Waiting period and survival period These two tenures may play a big role in whether you receive the benefits of the critical illness rider. Therefore, you may want to make sure that the waiting period and the survival period are reasonable and allow you to claim rider benefits while following policy terms and conditions. Conclusion A term insurance plan with a critical illness rider may be a great way to safeguard yourself and your family against the diagnosis of a critical illness or medical event. While you may not be able to predict if and when you may be diagnosed with an illness or require major surgery, the least you may be able to do is protect your finances against such an event. You may consult with a financial advisor so that you may take an appropriate decision for yourself and your loved ones.
